name: Theo & Henry Gillibrand
subject: Happy Mother's Day to our mom!
body:
| | |
---
Peter,
Hi! We're Theo and Henry Gillibrand (Kirsten's kids).
To all the moms out there: **Happy Mother 's Day!**
**[To celebrate our mom and how hard she's working to make our country better, we wanted to send her a Mother's Day card from all the people who are proud of her--including us! Can you sign? We think it would really mean a lot to her.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/kg2020-mothers-day?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3089-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fkg2020-mothers-day%3Frefcode%3Djtk3089-ja-gfp-nd")**
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/kg2020-mothers-day?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3089-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fkg2020-mothers-day%3Frefcode%3Djtk3089-ja-gfp-nd")
Do we think our mom would make an awesome president? Duh! When she told us she wanted to run, we were so excited, even though we knew it meant she would be away from home a lot.
We may be biased, but we don't just think she's the best because she's our mom. We know that the more people get to know her like we do, they'll see how strong and smart she is.
She really cares about everyone--even the other kids in our schools, and all the people she meets on the campaign. She's working really hard on issues we know are important for our country, like helping everyone get clean water, stopping school shootings and making sure we treat women the same as men. And we know she can beat Trump because she's way tougher than him.
We want her to have the best Mother's Day ever after everything she's trying to do for America.
**[Please sign her card now, and we're going to show it to her tonight!](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://action.kirstengillibrand.com/kg2020-mothers-day?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk3089-ja-gfp-nd&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fkg2020-mothers-day%3Frefcode%3Djtk3089-ja-gfp-nd")**
Thanks for supporting our mom.

--Theo and Henry

name: Our Revolution
subject: Supporting these women now could make them the next political superstars
body:
Our Revolution wants you to meet five progressive women who are running for office
# [](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a538/bed43eb/964a453a/75fb7f51/1184216038/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-header-OR")
Friends,
This Mother's Day weekend, Our Revolution wants you to meet five progressive women who are running for office and putting families first. Once you've been introduced, we're going to ask you to make a donation to fund Our Revolution's work electing them and more progressive candidates across the country.
[](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a538/bed43eb/964a453a/75fb7f50/1184216038/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-women?refcode=em190512snd&noskip=true")
_Tiffany Cab an, Helen Gym, Erika Almiron, Jackie Goldberg, and Yasmine Taeb_
**Tiffany Cab an** is the only public defender running for **Queens County District Attorney** on a basic premise: imprisonment should not be the first solution to social problems. Our society spends $143,000 per prisoner to lock them up. Tiffany wants to put that money toward jobs and education, not jails and incarceration.
Her race is a continuation of our fight to make the Empire State more just. Just last fall, we swept the so-called Independent Democratic Conference out of power in the New York Senate with 6 Our Revolution-endorsed state senators. Let's take the next step revolutionizing New York City and the criminal justice systems of these major American cities.
**Yasmine Taeb** is running for the **Virginia Senate** on a platform of protecting a woman's right to an abortion, the passage of the Equal Rights Amendment, and paid parental leave - an issue that her opponent ignores. Control of the Virginia Senate may swing to Democrats in this fall's election, and we need to make sure progressives are in power to drive the agenda in a unified state government - electing Yasmine is a critical piece of that mission.
**Helen Gym** and **Erika Almir on** are running for **Philadelphia City Council.**
Helen is an activist who co-founded Parents United for Public Education, and in her first term on the city council, she led the effort to pass a Fair Work Week law so that 130,000 workers could have stable schedules, a path to full-time work, and the opportunity to support themselves and their loved ones.
Erika is the daughter of house cleaners and a champion of the Domestic Workers' Bill of Rights, which includes overtime, paid sick leave, and fair contract protection. Together, Helen and Erika will give Philadelphia progressives a voice on the City Council _and_ the District Attorney's office with Larry Krasner, who has transformed Philadelphia's criminal justice system in part thanks to your help electing him as an Our Revolution candidate in 2017.
**Jackie Goldberg** is running for the **Los Angeles School Board** on a platform of promoting public schools and fighting for social services within educational institutions. In America's second largest city, electing champions for public schools will have an impact on millions of families.
Together, these candidates running for local and state government have the opportunity to affect change for millions of people in their communities. Putting your support behind local races is critical. We're not just going to elect a president, we're going to lead a political revolution.
**[Are you ready to help elect these women? Make a donation to Our Revolution today.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a538/bed43eb/964a453a/75fb7f50/1184216038/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-women?refcode=em190512snd&noskip=true")**
**[DONATE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a538/bed43eb/964a453a/75fb7f50/1184216038/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-women?refcode=em190512snd&noskip=true")**
Thanks for reading, and for your support of these extraordinary women.
In Solidarity,
The Team at Our Revolution

WASHINGTON, DC, 20035

name: Teresa Tomlinson
subject: Fighting for women in Georgia
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TERESA TOMLINSON FOR SENATE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tomlinsonforsenatelaunch?refcode=em_header [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tomlinsonforsenatelaunch?refcode=em_header]
Dear Friend –
My mother was 16 when she married my dad, who was 19. He was in the Air Force and she cared for his elderly parents and, then, worked in the meat department of grocery stores to help put dad through college. Her job was a union job that provided health benefits. My mom struggled with infertility, as did I. She tried to conceive for 10 years before I was born – her first. We both had the good fortune to have control over our reproductive health. Isn’t every woman entitled to that – good fortune or not?
https://teresatomlinson.com/stop-the-abortion-bans/?ms=em051219a [https://teresatomlinson.com/stop-the-abortion-bans/?ms=em051219a]
Love your mother; respect all women.
Apparently, that’s a lesson most white male Republican politicians in Georgia need to learn. This week, they decreed that women should be treated as vessels of the state, with no agency over our own bodies and health. The truth is women are the sole fiduciaries of any life we carry. Women make the intimate, sometimes difficult, decisions regarding our families and our reproductive health.
But now these male politicians have taken away the ability of Georgia women to choose if, when, and under what circumstances we will become mothers. They have placed their judgment over ours: they don't trust women and we shouldn't trust them with elected leadership.
Take one moment now to sign our petition opposing Governor Kemp’s rollback of the constitutional right to choose. [https://teresatomlinson.com/stop-the-abortion-bans/?ms=em051219a]
I won't stop fighting for women in Georgia, and women all over the country, as this stunt to set us back 50 years continues to spread to states across the country. These choice bans – and the criminalization of abortion and even miscarrying – are an attack on women and our health, safety, and equality.
Add your name now, and fight back against these abhorrent attacks on women in America. [https://teresatomlinson.com/stop-the-abortion-bans/?ms=em051219a]
All my life, I’ve stood up to bullies and fought for justice. These Republican men are bullying the women of our country and I’m not going to sit idly by and watch it happen. As Senator, I’ll fight for every person in Georgia, so that we all have the chance to make our own choices about our health, safety, and futures.
Thank you for fighting with me on behalf of women everywhere.
Truly yours,
Teresa
SIGN THE PETITION [https://teresatomlinson.com/stop-the-abortion-bans/?ms=em051219a]

name: Kamala Harris
subject: Momala
body:
This Mother’s Day, I’m taking a moment to reflect on becoming “Momala.”

I have had a lot of roles in my life, but the one I’m most proud of is
being a stepmother to Cole and Ella.

You can read my note below on our family and being Momala. But first, I
want to wish everyone a happy and restful Mother’s Day.

Thanks for all that you do.

— Kamala Harris

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/go/9446?t=1001&akid=4678%2E5755789%2E1m9E80 ]“Sen. Kamala Harris on Being 'Momala'”
Elle Magazine, May 10, 2019

When I met Doug, the man who would become my husband, I also met a man who
was a divorced father of two children, Cole and Ella, named after John
Coltrane and Ella Fitzgerald. As a child of divorce, I knew how hard it
could be when your parents start to date other people. And I was
determined not to insert myself in their lives until Doug and I had
established we were in this for the long haul. Children need consistency;
I didn’t want to insert myself into their lives as a temporary fixture
because I didn’t want to disappoint them. There’s nothing worse than
disappointing a child.

So, we took it slow, and Doug and I put a lot of thought into when and how
I would first meet the kids. And that meant waiting. As we waited,
anticipation grew. When the day finally came, I had butterflies in my
stomach. The plan was to go to a seafood hut off the Pacific Coast Highway
called the Reel Inn, a favorite of the kids.

On my way to meet Doug, I picked up a tin of cookies and tied a ribbon in
a bow around them. I took a few deep breaths. I was excited, and I was
nervous. I rehearsed what I would say. Would the kids think the cookies
were really nice or really weird? Was the ribbon too much? (The ribbon was
probably a little extra, but over the years Cole and Ella have spared me
by not telling me that.)

Cole and Ella could not have been more welcoming. They are brilliant,
talented, funny kids who have grown to be remarkable adults. I was already
hooked on Doug, but I believe it was Cole and Ella who reeled me in.

To know Cole and Ella is to know that their mother Kerstin is an
incredible mother. Kerstin and I hit it off ourselves and are dear
friends. She and I became a duo of cheerleaders in the bleachers at Ella’s
swim meets and basketball games, often to Ella’s embarrassment. We
sometimes joke that our modern family is almost a little too functional.

A few years later when Doug and I got married, Cole, Ella, and I agreed
that we didn’t like the term “stepmom.” Instead they came up with the name
“Momala.”

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/go/9446?t=1002&akid=4678%2E5755789%2E1m9E80 ]Continue reading Kamala’s note on Elle.com »

Kamala Harris is running for president because we deserve truth and
integrity from our elected leaders -- and that's not we're getting right
now. Pitch in today to support our historic campaign:

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

To know Cole and Ella is to know that their mother Kerstin is an incredible mother. Kerstin and I hit it off ourselves and are dear friends. She and I became a duo of cheerleaders in the bleachers at Ella’s swim meets and basketball games, often to Ella’s embarrassment. We sometimes joke that our modern family is almost a little too functional. A few years later when Doug and I got married, Cole, Ella, and I agreed that we didn’t like the term “stepmom.” Instead they came up with the name “Momala.” [ ]Continue reading Kamala’s note on Elle.com » Kamala Harris is running for president because we deserve truth and integrity from our elected leaders -- and that's not we're getting right now. name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: The story that's written on my heart
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Today, where mothers and families end up in life is about more than hard work and good fortune. The rules for a level playing field matter, too. That's what I'm fighting for to honor my mother, on Mot | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f9ea/45c30650/84c31b5c/669687f0/1281737033/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
I'm thinking about my mother today.

I was just a kid when my daddy had a heart attack. It was serious -- we weren't sure if he was going to make it. He eventually got better, but he was out of work for a long time.

We lost our family station wagon. It looked like the house would be next to go. At night, I'd overhear my parents talk, and that's when I learned words like "mortgage" and "foreclosure."

I remember one morning, I was standing in my mother's bedroom on a warm spring day. Her face was red and puffy, and about a dozen wadded-up tissues were on the bedspread next to a black dress. It was that one dress that every mom had -- the one that only came out for weddings, funerals, and graduations.

And she was saying, "We will not lose this house. We will not lose this house."

She was 50 years old. She had never worked outside the home. She was terrified.

I watched her while she pulled it together, put that dress on, put on her high heels, blew her nose, walked to Sears, and got a minimum wage job. And that minimum wage job saved our house, but more importantly, it saved our family.

**That story is who I am. That story is written on my heart.**

And for a long time, I used to think that was just a story about my mother.

But years later, I came to understand that it's a story about millions of Americans who -- no matter how scared they are -- reach down, dig deep, find it, and pull up what they need to take care of the family.

And then years later I came to understand this: **It 's also a story about government.**

Because when this happened to my family, a minimum wage job in America could support a family of three. It could take care of a mortgage, utilities, and put food on the table.

But today in America, a full-time minimum wage job will not keep a mama and a baby out of poverty. So let's be honest: My mother's story isn't possible today.

Right now, when people in government are deciding what the minimum wage should be, they're not trying to figure out what it takes to support a family -- to make it possible for people to get a little toe in the door.

No. I hear them in Washington. They ask, "What will improve the profitability of giant corporations?"

**I want a government that doesn't just work for giant corporations. I want one that works for little families like mine.**

My mother didn't have much, but she knew that her four kids had a fighting chance to build a future -- and that made her life successful.

Today, where mothers and families end up in life is about more than hard work and good fortune. The rules for a level playing field matter, too. That's what I'm fighting for to honor my mother, on Mother's Day and every day.

Thank you for being in this fight alongside me. Together, we reach down, dig deep, find the strength we need to find, fight for what we believe in -- and celebrate each other along the way.

Happy Mother's Day,

Elizabeth

subject: We’re not making the same mistake
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
Most people don’t know this -- but in past elections, campaigns have fallen short because they started their voter contact programs too late. We’re not making the same mistake. But to stay on track to elect Beto, we need more volunteers getting involved now.

#
Most people don’t know this -- but in past elections, campaigns have fallen short because they started their voter contact programs too late. We’re not making the same mistake. That’s why we were first out of the gate. But to stay on track to elect Beto, we need more volunteers getting involved _now_.
Our goal is to talk to more voters than any campaign for president in history. It’s an extremely ambitious goal, but we think it’s the right one: Talking to people face-to-face is proven to be one of the most effective ways to win votes. Months from now, we don’t want to look back and wish we’d done more earlier on.
[**Sign up to get started knocking on doors in your neighborhood. You can do it alone at a time that works for you, or bring a friend (or three!).**](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/canvass-for-beto/?source=ema20190512&t=1&akid=30717%2E444211%2EZ7tvxB) Volunteering to talk to voters now is the best way to help make sure we stay on track to elect Beto. It’s easy and fun, and we’ll walk you through all the steps.
**[Sign up »](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/canvass-for-beto/?source=ema20190512?source=ema20190512&t=2&akid=30717%2E444211%2EZ7tvxB)**
Nothing short of an unprecedented, grassroots effort is necessary to make sure we get Trump out of office and elect Beto. Waiting to make our voter contact program as large as we possibly can is not a chance we’re willing to take. That’s why we’re hustling hard now -- knocking on doors, talking to people, doing the work to move our country forward.
**We hope you’ll join us.[But if you can’t knock on doors now, please make a $3 contribution to help our hard-working field team grow at the level needed to win.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17006?refcode=ema20190512&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=30717%2E444211%2EZ7tvxB)**
Thank you,
Beto Organizing Team

name: Ed Markey
subject: Thinking about my mother
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Markey Committee
body:
| | | Friend,

This Mother's Day, like so many of you, I'm reflecting on my mother and all that she sacrificed for me and our family. She was valedictorian of her class. But after her mother died, her life trajectory changed.

This was before the New Deal, before Social Security. In those days, the only social safety net for families was one of the girls had to stay home.

So my mother had to abandon her college dreams, and she stayed home to care of her younger sisters.

My mother dreamed of a better life for her children - for me and my brothers. She saw the promise of hard work, education, and opportunity, helped by a government that worked for the people.

In her 80s, my mother developed Alzheimer's. My father took care of her in the living room of our Malden home for 13 years. She passed away in 1999, the same year I founded the bipartisan Congressional Taskforce on Alzheimer's disease.

In 2010, my legislation -- the National Alzheimer's Project Act -- became law. It represents the federal government's boldest commitment to tackling the disease, with the goal for preventing or treating Alzheimer's by 2025. It also represents my commitment to my mother to help find a cure, and to honor her and all that I learned from her dedication and love.

On this Mother's Day, I wanted to share my gratitude for my mother's example of determination and hard work, and give thanks to friends and supporters like you who have fueled our efforts for change and progress to end this terrible disease.

I hope this Mother's Day is filled with joy, and fond memories, and inspiration for all of you.

Thanks,
Ed

name: =?utf-8?Q?Dr.=20Joan=20Perry=20for=20Congress?=
subject: Happy Mother's Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Joan Perry for Congress, All rights reserved
body:
Whether you're a mother, wishing to be one, or taking care of your mother, I hope this video will encourage you. | | | | [ ](https://drjoanperry.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=40b941504fba213abdaddc0bc&id=16ab144a00&e=0bf1d0f47f)
---
| | | Peter Willis,

Happy Mother’s Day! I hope you’re enjoying the day with those you love. You may not realize this, but this is my first Mother’s Day without my mom. If you’re missing your mother, you’re longing to be a mother, or you have a difficult relationship with your mother, I want to acknowledge how difficult this day may be for you.

I’m grateful for my mother and the impact she had in my life. After my father died, my mother and her little dog Sally moved in with us for 10 years, until mom passed away in December. We certainly miss her.

In fact, when I was asked to speak at my alma mater for the Morehead-Cain Alumni Forum, I shared about my experience as mom's primary caregiver. If you are caring for an aging parent, I hope [this video](https://drjoanperry.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=40b941504fba213abdaddc0bc&id=8e313b8dc1&e=0bf1d0f47f) of my presentation will encourage you today.

Becoming a mother was always a dream of mine, but my path into motherhood was quite different than I imagined. Jimbo and I were married on October 24, 1981 and I discovered I was pregnant a few months later during my surgery rotation in medical school at UNC-Chapel Hill. Since doctors had warned me I would likely struggle with infertility, my pregnancy came as quite a shock.

>From my schooling point of view, this could not have come at a worse time, but from God’s point of view, it was perfect timing. _God knows best_. I’ve come to realize many of life’s greatest blessings are unplanned. We welcomed our son Madison into the world on October 1, 1982.

Friends who know our love of sports, especially basketball, say our motivation for having five children was to create our own basketball team: Team Perry. While that’s not true, in addition to Madison, I’m grateful God blessed us with Stockton, Stephen, Nathan, and Jackson.

I love being a mom and a grandmother!

For those of you who are mothers, I want you to realize your hard work as a mother is valuable work. From my vantage point now, I realize the truth of parenting, “the days are long but the years are short.”

May God bless you today.

Joan

P.S. It’s been a wonderful week filled with endorsements and great progress, keep your eyes on your inbox, more great news is coming your way.

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: This problem is a GLOBAL problem
body:
To effectively oppose right-wing authoritarianism, we cannot simply go back to the failed status quo of the last several decades.
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1328%2E150750%2EB1fI0l)

Friends -
One of the great crises facing the global community today is that democracy, the right of ordinary people to control their own lives, is on the defensive while authoritarianism is growing stronger.
And at its root is the fact that a handful of incredibly wealthy people are exerting enormous economic and political power over the planet. Unbelievably, in the global economy today, the top 1 percent owns more wealth than the bottom 99 percent, and a handful of billionaires own more than the bottom half of people around the world — that’s 3.7 **_billion**_ people.
That is the reality.
People in our own country, and around the world, are angry and betrayed, and they feel that nobody is listening to their pain.
**And one of the results of that reality is that in Europe, in Russia, in the Middle East, in Asia and elsewhere we are seeing movements led by demagogues who exploit people’s fears, prejudices and grievances to achieve and hold on to power.**
Next week, Donald Trump is set to welcome one of those leaders into the White House: Hungary’s far-right authoritarian Prime Minister, Viktor Orbán.
Now, I have always found it very strange that Trump has such a hard time getting along with leaders of the world’s major democracies but feels very comfortable with authoritarians like Orbán, Putin, Xi Jinping, Bolsonaro and Mohammad bin Salman.
But the truth is, while they all differ in some respects, they share a number of key attributes: hostility toward democratic norms, antagonism toward a free press, intolerance toward ethnic and religious minorities, and a belief that government should benefit their own selfish financial interests.
This trend certainly did not begin with Trump, but there’s no question that authoritarian leaders around the world have drawn inspiration from the fact that the leader of the world’s oldest and most powerful democracy seems to delight in shattering democratic norms.
Other authoritarian states are much farther along this kleptocratic process. In Russia, it is impossible to tell where the decisions of government end and the interests of Vladimir Putin and his circle of oligarchs begin. They operate as one unit. Similarly, in Saudi Arabia, there is no debate about separation because the natural resources of the state, valued at trillions of dollars, belong to the Saudi royal family. In Hungary, far-right authoritarian leader Viktor Orbán is openly allied with Putin in Russia. In China, an inner circle led by Xi Jinping has steadily consolidated power, clamping down on domestic political freedom while it aggressively promotes a version of authoritarian capitalism abroad.
So the question is: Where do we go from here?
**To effectively oppose right-wing authoritarianism, we cannot simply go back to the failed status quo of the last several decades. In order to fight this trend, we need to strengthen the global coalition of progressive democrats.**
While authoritarians promote division and hatred, we promote unity, inclusion, and an agenda based on economic, social, racial, and environmental justice.
The people of the world must come together to end the absurdity of rich and multinational corporations stashing over $21 trillion in offshore bank accounts to avoid paying their fair share of taxes and then demanding that their respective governments impose an austerity agenda on their working families.
It is not acceptable that the fossil fuel industry continues to make huge profits while their carbon emissions destroy the planet for our children and grandchildren.
It is not acceptable that a handful of multinational media giants, owned by a small number of billionaires, largely control the flow of information on the planet.
It is not acceptable that trade policies that benefit large multinational corporations and encourage a race to the bottom hurt working people throughout the world as they are written out of public view.
It is not acceptable that, with the Cold War long behind us, countries around the world spend over $1 trillion a year on weapons of destruction, while millions of children die of easily treatable diseases.
In order to effectively combat the rise of the international authoritarian axis, we need a global progressive movement that mobilizes behind a vision of shared prosperity, security and dignity for all people and that addresses the massive inequality that exists, not only in wealth but in political power as well.
Such a movement must be willing to think creatively and boldly about the world that we would like to see.
We must take the opportunity to reconceptualize a genuinely progressive global community based on human solidarity, that recognizes that every person on this planet shares a common humanity, that we all want our children to grow up healthy, to have a good education, have decent jobs, drink clean water, breathe clean air, and live in peace.
Our job is to reach out to those in every corner of the world who share these values and who are fighting for a better world.
**In a time of exploding wealth and technology, we have the potential to create a decent life for all people. Our job is to build on our common humanity and do everything that we can to oppose all of the forces, whether unaccountable government power or unaccountable corporate power, who try to divide us up and set us against each other.**
We know that those forces work together across borders. We must do the same.
Thank you for reading.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

* * *

**In this campaign, we are taking on very powerful interests who will do and spend whatever it takes to stop us. Can you please chip in to help us close the gap on Trump’s massive fundraising advantage before the next FEC deadline comes to a close?**
[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190512-1-30&t=2&akid=1328%2E150750%2EB1fI0l)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=3&akid=1328%2E150750%2EB1fI0l). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=4&akid=1328%2E150750%2EB1fI0l). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=5&refcode2=1328_150750_B1fI0l&akid=1328%2E150750%2EB1fI0l).
Submit this form to [update your contact information](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/update-info/?t=6&akid=1328%2E150750%2EB1fI0l).
